title: rubus × pubescens
intro: Rubus × pubescens, also known as the dewberry or thimbleberry, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It is native to North America and is characterized by its thimble-shaped fruit and pubescent leaves. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of anthocyanins and flavonoids. The health benefits of Rubus × pubescens include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antioxidant, and it is often prepared as a tea, jam, or supplement to harness its medicinal properties.
